title: Simpsoncalifragilisticexpiala Annoyed Gr

jokes.text: "K.K.K."? That's not good. [Nervous Laugh]
jokes.setup: Krusty announces 'Krusty Komedy Klassic' with the abbreviation K.K.K.
jokes.punchline: He realizes the unfortunate abbreviation matches the KKK hate group
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor that catches an obvious but unnoticed problem. Perfect economy and character-appropriate panic response.

jokes.text: Here's a dog that's been trained to catch this red rubber ball. [Yells] Oh! Somebody shoot it. Somebody shoot it.
jokes.setup: Krusty introduces a simple dog trick as revolutionary TV
jokes.punchline: The trick goes wrong and he desperately calls for violence against the dog
jokes.explanation: Classic Krusty desperation humor with absurd escalation from simple trick to calling for violence

jokes.text: These specials get worse every year.
jokes.setup: We've just watched terrible Krusty comedy
jokes.punchline: Homer's meta-commentary on declining TV quality
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor that lets audience agree with Homer's assessment of what we just saw

jokes.text: Hello? Hello? Stupid cordless phone. I'll try the old-fashioned model.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to use the TV remote as a phone
jokes.punchline: He blames the 'cordless phone' for not working
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity - confusing remote with phone but logically explaining the failure

jokes.text: Mad About Shoe
jokes.setup: Parody of popular sitcom 'Mad About You'
jokes.punchline: Changed to 'Mad About Shoe' for no logical reason
jokes.explanation: Lazy wordplay that substitutes random word. Typical of bad TV parody but intentionally bad

jokes.text: You're not gonna like our 'NYPD Shoe' sketch. It's pretty much the same thing.
jokes.setup: Another TV show parody following the shoe pattern
jokes.punchline: Honest admission that they're just repeating the same lazy joke
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor acknowledging the lazy writing, which makes it self-aware rather than just bad

jokes.text: No, that's okay. I'll just go without liquid. [Gagging]
jokes.setup: Bart asks for milk, Marge resists
jokes.punchline: Bart's dramatic martyrdom over not getting milk, complete with gagging
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart manipulation through theatrical suffering, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Homer immediately asks for beer after Marge caves to Bart
jokes.setup: Marge gives in to Bart's manipulation and offers to get things for everyone
jokes.punchline: Homer takes advantage despite everyone else saying no
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer selfishness - perfectly in character to pile on when Marge is already stressed

jokes.text: Um, there's a hair in my soup- but I'll just eat around it.
jokes.setup: Lisa finds hair in soup
jokes.punchline: Her polite willingness to eat around it rather than complain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - too polite to properly complain about disgusting situation

jokes.text: Well, all the good presidents turned us down.
jokes.setup: Krusty thanks Gerald Ford for appearing
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that Ford was their last choice after better presidents refused
jokes.explanation: Brutal deadpan insult delivered as casual aside. Perfect economy and unexpectedly harsh

jokes.text: Oh! Uh, how's your wife Nancy? - Betty. - Who cares?
jokes.setup: Krusty tries to make small talk with wrong wife's name
jokes.punchline: When corrected, he immediately abandons the pretense of caring
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty rudeness - doesn't even try to maintain the facade once called out

jokes.text: I was just watching women's volleyball on ESPN.
jokes.setup: Homer needs excuse for being away from upset Marge
jokes.punchline: Transparent lie about watching 'respectable' sports
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer excuse-making, implies he was watching something inappropriate

jokes.text: There's no need for that 'baba-ma-bushka.'
jokes.setup: Marge is wearing head covering
jokes.punchline: Homer's mispronunciation of 'babushka' as nonsense word
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer malapropism that's endearing rather than mean-spirited

jokes.text: Homer calls Marge's babushka 'baba-ma-bushka'
jokes.setup: Marge wearing head covering due to hair loss
jokes.punchline: Homer's mispronunciation/malapropism of babushka
jokes.explanation: Mild Homer wordplay. Shows his character but the joke itself is weak

jokes.text: Oh, there's no way I could- [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer confidently says he won't be shocked by Marge's appearance
jokes.punchline: Immediate terrified scream upon seeing her
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/subversion - perfect timing between confidence and terror

jokes.text: I'll teach you to comb it over so no one can tell- Just like my hair.
jokes.setup: Homer offers to help Marge hide hair loss
jokes.punchline: References his own obviously terrible combover
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness to his own bad hair makes his advice ironically worthless

jokes.text: This thing has me buffaloed. [Laughs]
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert admits he's puzzled by Marge's condition
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate laugh after using dated expression
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Hibbert inappropriate laughter at his own corniness

jokes.text: And I'll take up smoking, and give that up.
jokes.setup: Family discussing sacrifices to pay for nanny
jokes.punchline: Bart's plan to start bad habit just to quit it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic from Bart - perfect economy and unexpected solution to making sacrifice

jokes.text: Giving up smoking is one of the hardest things you'll ever have to do. Have a dollar.
jokes.setup: Homer responds to Bart's smoking plan
jokes.punchline: Treats it as real accomplishment and rewards him
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness creates perfect callback - he genuinely believes Bart accomplished something

jokes.text: But he didn't do anything. Didn't he, Lisa? Didn't he?
jokes.setup: Lisa points out Bart did nothing
jokes.punchline: Homer's insistent defense of rewarding nothing
jokes.explanation: Homer doubling down on his absurd logic against Lisa's reason

jokes.text: Well, Homer's out. We gotta find a new General Ambrose Burnside.
jokes.setup: Homer gives up Civil War reenactment for nanny money
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of replacing him in very specific historical role
jokes.explanation: Absurdity of Homer playing specific Civil War general makes his departure seem significant

jokes.text: The South shall come again.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Civil War reenactors
jokes.punchline: Mispronunciation of 'rise again' as 'come again'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that undermines the dramatic Confederate sentiment

jokes.text: I saw Mrs. Doubtfire. This is a man in drag! You're a phony! Fakey, phony, fraud!
jokes.setup: Elderly woman applicant appears at door
jokes.punchline: Homer's paranoid accusation based on movie reference
jokes.explanation: Homer's movie-influenced paranoia leads to aggressive alliteration and false accusation

jokes.text: I'll keep a watchful eye on your kids... and if they get out of line- Pow!
jokes.setup: Tough-looking male nanny describes his approach
jokes.punchline: Threatens violence against children as selling point
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in inappropriate nanny approach - threatens what he's supposed to protect

jokes.text: I like him.
jokes.setup: Violent nanny threatens to hit kids
jokes.punchline: Bart approves of the threatening approach
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart response - approves of chaos and rebellion even when directed at him

jokes.text: Hey, where do you keep the liquor? I hide a bottle of schnapps in the baby's crib.
jokes.setup: Sketchy nanny asks about alcohol
jokes.punchline: Homer casually reveals inappropriate alcohol hiding place
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in Homer's casual admission of endangering baby with alcohol storage

jokes.text: You're telling me, you blue-haired witch.
jokes.setup: Marge rejects the violent nanny applicant
jokes.punchline: He insults her on the way out
jokes.explanation: Predictable angry response but the specific 'blue-haired witch' insult is memorable

jokes.text: Just cuttin' through the treacle.
jokes.setup: Bart interrupts the overly sweet musical number
jokes.punchline: British slang for 'cutting through the BS'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - breaks fourth wall to comment on saccharine content while using clever British slang

jokes.text: Did you say Mary Pop- No. I definitely did not.
jokes.setup: Marge almost says 'Mary Poppins' to obvious lookalike
jokes.punchline: Shary's aggressive denial of copyright infringement
jokes.explanation: Sharp legal parody - character knows she's a ripoff and aggressively denies it

jokes.text: I'm an original creation, like Ricky Rouse and Monald Muck.
jokes.setup: Shary denies being Mary Poppins ripoff
jokes.punchline: References obvious Mickey Mouse and Donald Duck knockoffs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about copyright avoidance with perfect fake character names. Economy and wit are exceptional.

jokes.text: Put me down for one of each.
jokes.setup: Shary offers to tell stories and change diapers
jokes.punchline: Homer treats adult services like ordering from menu
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal interpretation creates inappropriate implication about adult diaper changing

jokes.text: No, I'm practically perfect in every way. Well, so am I. [Belches]
jokes.setup: Shary claims perfection, Homer agrees about himself
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately belches, disproving his claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - Homer's claim of perfection immediately undercut by crude behavior

jokes.text: Come on. Isn't he the guy I bowl with? The black guy. That's Carl.
jokes.setup: Discussion of fancy British employers
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks Lord Huffington is his bowling buddy Carl
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete lack of worldliness - thinks British lord is his coworker

jokes.text: I make you read every article in that magazine... including Norman Mailer's latest claptrap about his waning libido.
jokes.setup: Shary responds to Bart's pornography test
jokes.punchline: Cruel punishment of making him read boring intellectual content in Playboy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant punishment that acknowledges adult magazines have pretentious writing. Very sophisticated humor.

jokes.text: Her butt waxed the bannister. Ooh! I can see myself.
jokes.setup: Shary slides down bannister
jokes.punchline: Homer notices the polishing effect and admires his reflection
jokes.explanation: Homer's shallow focus on personal benefit from magical event is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Shary Bobbins and I were engaged to be wed back in the old country. Then she got her eyesight back.
jokes.setup: Willie reveals romantic history with Shary
jokes.punchline: Her restored vision ended their relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with excellent self-deprecating humor from Willie. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: Suddenly the ugliest man in Glasgow wasn't good enough for her.
jokes.setup: Willie's self-deprecation about eyesight
jokes.punchline: Claims to be ugliest in entire city
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates Willie's self-deprecation to absurd citywide scale

jokes.text: Boy for sale. Boy for sale. Is this legal, man? Only here and in Mississippi.
jokes.setup: Victorian fantasy sequence with boy being sold
jokes.punchline: Legal only in backwards location (Mississippi)
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about child slavery with regional stereotype punchline

jokes.text: Balderdash. This is the silliest load of- Oh! Look at it fly! [Giggling] Look at me, Smithers. I feel practically super-duper, fragically-
jokes.setup: Burns dismisses kite flying as silly
jokes.punchline: Immediately converts to childlike enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Burns' capacity for joy despite his evil nature. Perfect reversal.

jokes.text: What's this strange sensation in my chest? I think your heart's beating again.
jokes.setup: Burns feels unusual sensation
jokes.punchline: His heart literally wasn't beating before
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor implying Burns is literally heartless. Perfect economy and character truth.

jokes.text: It's so full and thick it can support a beach umbrella.
jokes.setup: Marge's hair has grown back
jokes.punchline: Absurd description of hair thickness with practical application
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of hair thickness to absurd practical level with visual component

jokes.text: Oh! Ooh- No, no. Leave it in.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge in bed with beach umbrella
jokes.punchline: Marge wants to keep umbrella during intimacy
jokes.explanation: Callback to hair joke with implied sexual situation made absurd by umbrella

jokes.text: I've been singing you songs all day. I'm not a bloody jukebox.
jokes.setup: Kids ask for another song
jokes.punchline: Mary Poppins character breaks and swears
jokes.explanation: Perfect character break moment - wholesome nanny suddenly shows human frustration with mild profanity

jokes.text: The secret ingredient is blood.
jokes.setup: Homer enjoys blood pudding
jokes.punchline: Shary matter-of-factly explains the literal name
jokes.explanation: Simple dark humor explaining disgusting British food with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Simpsons immediately revert: Bart attacking Lisa with snarling
jokes.setup: Shary thinking her work is done
jokes.punchline: Kids immediately return to violent behavior the moment she turns away
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy showing how quickly the Simpsons return to dysfunction

jokes.text: I think we got our umbrellas switched! Whee! I never felt so alive!
jokes.setup: Shary tries to fly away with umbrella
jokes.punchline: Gets wrong umbrella, falls, but enjoys the experience
jokes.explanation: Good callback to umbrella joke with character's positive attitude despite failure

jokes.text: [Austrian Rainier Wolfcastle commercial in German]
jokes.setup: TV show about celebrities before fame
jokes.punchline: Action hero doing silly Austrian commercial
jokes.explanation: Standard 'before they were famous' parody showing action star in embarrassing early work

jokes.text: You'll find it's even more fun if you get it for me.
jokes.setup: Homer demands beer, Shary tries to make it fun
jokes.punchline: Suggests he'll enjoy serving her instead
jokes.explanation: Mild attempt at magical positivity that fails against Homer's selfishness

jokes.text: Lady, the man asked for a beer, not a song.
jokes.setup: Shary tries to sing instead of getting beer
jokes.punchline: Barney's blunt rejection of musical solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney moment - cuts through BS with alcohol-focused practicality

jokes.text: Where's Otis? He's not in his cell. I shot him. Well, that's a-What? Now I'm goin' down to Emmett's fix-it shop- to fix Emmett.
jokes.setup: Parody of Andy Griffith Show with Charles Bronson
jokes.punchline: Violent solutions to small-town problems
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody combining Bronson's violent persona with wholesome Mayberry setting. Perfect escalation from shooting to 'fixing' people.

jokes.text: I got a better game. It's called whippin' cupcakes.
jokes.setup: Shary suggests cleaning can be a game
jokes.punchline: Bart prefers violent food fight game
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart rejection of authority with preference for destructive alternative

jokes.text: What I'm trying to say in this cartoon... is that violence is everywhere in our society- You know, it's, like, even in breakfast cereals, man.
jokes.setup: Violent cartoon ends
jokes.punchline: Pretentious director explanation of obvious violence
jokes.explanation: Sharp parody of Tarantino's tendency to intellectualize his violence with pseudo-profound commentary

jokes.text: Been there, done it.
jokes.setup: Shary suggests exploring the big world
jokes.punchline: Lisa's jaded dismissal of worldly experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - precocious child acting world-weary beyond her years

jokes.text: So you like it this way? Indubitably.
jokes.setup: Shary asks if family likes being dysfunctional
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sophisticated affirmative response
jokes.explanation: Lisa's vocabulary showcasing her intelligence while embracing family dysfunction

jokes.text: So long, Superman.
jokes.setup: Family says goodbye to flying Shary
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual comparison to Superman
jokes.explanation: Bart's matter-of-fact acceptance of magical nanny as superhero figure

tmdb_overview: Marge is stressed by the demands of motherhood and the Simpsons get a magical British nanny, who floats down from the sky holding an umbrella.
insight: Mary Poppins parody sustains 86.5 score across 55 jokes with escalating absurdist chaos.
